chief_complaint: 66-year-old woman presented with dizziness and speech impairment lasting 1 day.
history: The patient initially presented with stroke-mimicking onset symptoms. MRI revealed subtle diffusion restriction at the corticomedullary junction. Initially, acute ischemic stroke was considered, and antiplatelet, cerebral circulation improvement, and neurotrophic therapies administered.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: MRI revealed subtle diffusion restriction at the corticomedullary junction. NOTCH2NLC gene testing revealed 104 GGC repeats in the 5'UTR region. Skin pathology demonstrated intranuclear inclusions.
